proxy.description: The proxy is not active. Click the button below to activate it.
proxy.inactive: Activate the proxy to see blocked requests

update.updateAvailable: A new version of Zen is available.

appRoutingSettings.description: Choose which apps should use Zen's proxy.
appRoutingSettings.emptyAllowlistWarning: No apps are selected. All traffic will bypass Zen.
appRoutingSettings.label: App routing
appRoutingSettings.saveError: Failed to save app routing settings: {{error}}

assetPortInput.description: The port used by the internal asset server for injecting scripts and styles.
assetPortInput.helper: Using a port below 1024 may require elevated privileges.
assetPortInput.label: Asset server port
assetPortInput.setError: Failed to set asset server port: {{error}}

autoStartSwitch.description: Start Zen automatically when you log in
autoStartSwitch.label: Autostart

common.stopProxyToAddFilter: Stop the proxy to add a new filter list
common.stopProxyToDeleteFilter: Stop the proxy to delete this filter list
common.stopProxyToEditRules: Stop the proxy to modify custom rules
common.stopProxyToModify: Stop the proxy to modify this setting
common.stopProxyToToggleFilter: Stop the proxy to enable/disable this filter list

createFilterList.trustedListsWarning: Trusted lists can use powerful blocking capabilities, such as trusted- scriptlets and JavaScript rules but may disrupt privacy and security if hijacked by a malicious party. Only enable this option for lists from sources you trust.

ignoredHostsInput.description: Hosts to exclude from proxying. Use this for services with certificate pinning or those disrupted by proxying.
ignoredHostsInput.label: Ignored hosts

connect.caNote:
On first start, Zen may prompt you to install a local root CA certificate. This enables HTTPS filtering so Zen can block ads and trackers on all sites. Everything happens locally – no data ever leaves your device.
See our GitHub for full technical details.
connect.description: You're ready to go. Press "Start" to enable Zen's comprehensive network protection.
connect.socialText: If you'd like, check out our socials:
connect.title: All set!

filterLists.description: To enable ad-blocking and privacy protection, Zen uses filter lists. You can also set them up later in the app.
filterLists.recommendation: Based on your locale, we recommend enabling the following filter lists:
filterLists.title: Filter lists

settings.settingsNote: You can also change these at any time in the app.
settings.title: Settings

portInput.description: The port the proxy server will listen on (0 for random).
portInput.helper: Using a port below 1024 may require elevated privileges.
portInput.label: Port

requestLog.emptyState: Start browsing to see blocked requests.

about.tagline: Your Comprehensive Ad-Blocker and Privacy Guard

ca.confirmTitle: Are you sure you want to uninstall the CA?

reasons.malfunctioning: You suspect that your CA installation is malfunctioning. This issue may manifest as a browser error when visiting HTTPS websites, or as other applications being unable to connect to the internet.
reasons.uninstall: You want to uninstall Zen completely.

ca.reinstallInfo: If you start Zen again, a new CA will be installed.
ca.stopProxyTooltip: Stop the proxy to uninstall the CA

updates.description: When enabled, Zen will automatically check for updates

startStopButton.unsupportedDEGuide:
Automatic system proxy configuration is currently only supported on GNOME and KDE.
Please follow this guide to set the proxy on a per-app basis.
